facts_disclosed: Moved cart to Soi Sukhumvit 38 near Thonglor BTS — new condo development brought more customers, 350 plates/day, 1,500 baht more rent per month

turns.content: GUESS WHAT na ka!! I have the BIGGEST news. you will not believe what I did 😆😆
Okay your energy is through the roof right now so this must be good! Tell me everything — what did you do??

I MOVED MY CART!! 🎉🎉 I am now at Soi Sukhumvit 38 near Thonglor BTS na ka!! brand new spot. there are so many new condos going up around there and my friend who sell som tum told me 'Pan come here the lunch crowd is insane.' she was right — office people and condo people EVERYWHERE and not enough good food carts

Thonglor area! That's a serious upgrade in foot traffic. New condo developments always bring a wave of hungry workers and residents who need good food nearby. How did you actually get a spot there though? Prime locations near BTS stations are competitive.

I got lucky na ka!! another vendor was retiring and leaving her spot. so I take over. the rent is 1,500 baht more per month than what I was paying before but arai wa it is SO worth it because guess how many plates I sell now??
Alright I'm ready — hit me with the number!

350!! THREE HUNDRED FIFTY na ka!! 350 plates a day!! I almost cry when I count last week. I was doing 200 before and thought that was good. now 350 and the line go from 11am to 1:30pm non-stop. I wake up 3:30am now to prep enough chicken and rice haha

350 plates a day — that's a 75% increase! The extra 150 plates per day more than pays for 1,500 baht in additional rent. At your prices that's thousands of extra baht daily. Was it hard to leave your old spot emotionally though? You were there a long time.

honestly na ka... I cry leaving Soi Rangnam. 4 years at that spot. my regular customers, the other vendors who are like family, the security guard uncle who always watch my cart when I go toilet haha. I cry real tears on the last day. but business is business na ka. I cannot be emotional and lose this opportunity

Four years of memories and bonds — of course you cried. That security guard uncle watching your cart is the kind of community that makes street food special. But going from 200 to 350 plates for just 1,500 baht more in rent is objectively a brilliant business move. Do your old regulars know where you went?

yes!! I post on Instagram about the move and some old regulars actually come to Thonglor to eat na ka. one aunty take the BTS from Victory Monument to Thonglor just for my khao man gai!! I almost cry again 😭 when people travel across Bangkok for your food that is the best compliment

An aunty riding the BTS across town for your chicken rice — that says more than any food blogger review ever could. Between your loyal old customers making the trip and a whole new neighbourhood of 350 daily customers, you're in an amazing position right now.

I feel so blessed na ka. sometimes I stand at my new spot and look at the line and think 'my grandmother would not believe this.' 3 generations of this recipe and now 350 plates a day near Thonglor BTS. best decision I ever make. okay I need to go prep — 350 plates don't cook themselves haha 🙏

Your grandmother would be incredibly proud — 3 generations later and the recipe is feeding 350 people a day in one of Bangkok's busiest areas. You earned this by having the courage to make the move. Not everyone would leave a comfortable 4-year spot, but you trusted the numbers and it paid off. Go prep those plates! 🙏
